data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260507T233944-084dbb37/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260507T233944-084dbb37/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260507T233944-084dbb37/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260507T233944-084dbb37/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260507T233944-084dbb37/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 3. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.

  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-7cb3153d">
    <title>agent: create project-root candidate refs with retention policy</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
Candidate commits created in a temp worktree must be resolvable from the project repository before checks, review, repair, landing, or crash recovery can rely on them. Today the worker returns a raw result_rev from the temp worktree, but no dedicated project-root candidate ref lifecycle exists for the FEAT-010 candidate-cycle pipeline.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:32-103 defines ExecuteBeadResult with base_rev/result_rev/preserve_ref fields but no candidate_ref or cycle_index metadata.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:397-410 in FEAT-010's sequence says candidate refs must be pinned before candidate checks and read-only review, but the loop currently goes from implementation result toward checks/review/land without a project-root candidate ref record.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:696-708 creates land-time preserve refs under refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<attempt-id>-<short-tip>, but that only covers failed/deferred landing; it is not the per-cycle candidate ref required before checks/review.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:333-365 defines each cycle as recording cycle_index, attempt_id, base_rev, result_rev, and a candidate ref under refs/ddx/iterations/<attempt-id>/<cycle-index>.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add CandidateRefStore or equivalent agent package helper that pins each close-eligible candidate commit in the project repo under refs/ddx/iterations/<attempt-id>/<cycle-index> before candidate checks and read-only review run.
- Use cycle_index starting at 0 for the first implementation cycle in an attempt and increment for each append-only repair cycle.
- Record candidate_ref and cycle_index alongside existing attempt metadata. The concrete storage target is ExecuteBeadResult/result.json (add JSON fields) plus the append-only bead event emitted for the candidate-cycle step; include base_rev and result_rev so the cycle can be reconstructed after worktree cleanup.
- Retention policy: landed temporary candidate refs may be deleted only after result.json and the bead event/landed evidence containing candidate_ref, base_rev, result_rev, attempt_id, and cycle_index are durable in the project branch. Preserved/manual/parked/conflicted candidates retain the candidate ref for operator inspection and recovery.
- Ensure git -C <projectRoot> rev-parse <candidate_ref> resolves even after the temp worktree is removed.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not move review or repair into the worktree yet.
- Do not implement stale ref pruning beyond the retention behavior above.
- Do not rename existing land-time preserve refs in execute_bead_land.go except where tests need to distinguish preserve_ref from candidate_ref.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-31e07fdc. Depends on ddx-49d8d6cb for coordinator interfaces.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. CandidateRefStore or equivalent pins candidate commits under refs/ddx/iterations/<attempt-id>/<cycle-index> before candidate checks and read-only review run.
2. TestCandidateRefStore_ProjectRootReachable verifies git -C <projectRoot> rev-parse <candidate_ref> succeeds for a candidate produced in a temp worktree, including after that temp worktree is removed.
3. TestCandidateRefStore_MetadataRecorded verifies result.json / ExecuteBeadResult includes candidate_ref and cycle_index plus existing base_rev, result_rev, and attempt_id for the cycle.
4. TestCandidateRefStore_EventRecorded verifies the bead event emitted for the candidate-cycle step includes candidate_ref, cycle_index, base_rev, result_rev, and attempt_id.
5. TestCandidateRefStore_RetentionPolicy verifies approved-landed temporary refs are cleaned only after candidate metadata is durable, while preserved/manual/parked/conflicted candidates retain refs.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestCandidateRefStore" green.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:agent, area:git, kind:feature, reliability, adr:024, spec:FEAT-010, candidate-cycle</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-31e07fdc" spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="d8a01b088cf84c10d7208784482874b0c73cd385" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260507T233944-084dbb37"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
All 4 `TestCandidateRefStore` tests pass. Committed as `8beb196b8` with `[ddx-7cb3153d]`.

**Summary of what was delivered:**

- **`GitCandidateRefStore`** (`candidate_cycle.go`): concrete `CandidateRefStore` using `git update-ref` to pin refs under `refs/ddx/iterations/<attempt-id>/<cycle-index>` in the project root — survives temp worktree removal
- **`ShouldRetainCandidateRef`**: retention policy — `success` → clean up; preserved/conflicted/parked/manual → retain
- **`CandidateCycleEventBody`** + `candidate_cycle_pinned` bead event emission wired into `AttemptCycleCoordinator.Run`
- **`AttemptCycleCoordinator`** extended with `ProjectRoot` and `BeadEvents` fields; `Run` now pins before checks/review, propagates `CandidateRef`/`CycleIndex` to landed report, and unpins or retains per retention policy
- **`ExecuteBeadResult`** + **`ExecuteBeadReport`**: `candidate_ref` and `cycle_index` JSON fields added
- **4 tests** covering all ACs (AC2–AC5), all green
